cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the creek path with the kids — it's flat, shaded, and easy enough for little legs. Bring bikes if you have them.
cards.why: Cool morning air and a peaceful trail is the best way to kick off the day.

cards.name: Santana Row Farmers Market
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose
cards.blurb: Wander Santana Row Farmers Market and grab lunch from the vendors. Good spot to let the kids pick out a snack or treat while you browse.
cards.why: Fresh air, easy food, and no commitments — perfect midday stop for a family.

cards.name: Vasona Lake County Park
cards.address: 333 Blossom Hill Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Vasona Lake for the lake views and easy trails, and if the kids spot the little train, that's basically the whole day sorted for them.
cards.why: Kids absolutely love this park and there's enough here to keep everyone happy for a couple hours.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Walk through the Triton Museum of Art and let the kids loose in the sculpture garden. The galleries are free and the outdoor space gives them room to roam.
cards.why: Free admission and a sculpture garden makes this a surprisingly great family stop.

cards.name: Kids Knitting (Ages 7+)
cards.address: 110 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030
cards.blurb: Drop by the Los Gatos Library for Kids Knitting — it's free and open to ages 7 and up. Great for any kid who likes making things with their hands.
cards.why: A free hands-on activity for kids is hard to pass up, especially on a weekday.

cards.name: Olalá Colombian ice cream
cards.address: 475 E Campbell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Stop at Olalá for Colombian ice cream on Campbell Ave. Let the kids pick a flavor and sit outside while everyone winds down from the afternoon.
cards.why: It's a fun, low-key treat stop that kids will ask to come back to.

cards.name: Puffin Donuts
cards.address: 1442 Pollard Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Start the day with a fresh donut and coffee at Puffin Donuts — grab a classic glazed or whatever's looking good in the case.
cards.why: Best way to kick off a Wednesday morning in Los Gatos.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Walk or bike the Los Gatos Creek Trail and enjoy the shade and creek views — it's an easy, relaxed stretch that feels surprisingly removed from the suburbs.
cards.why: Cool crisp morning temps make this the perfect window for a trail walk.

cards.name: Los Gatos Cafe Uptown
cards.address: 15662 Los Gatos Blvd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Los Gatos Cafe Uptown for a solid sit-down lunch before they close at 2 — order something from the brunch-lunch menu and take your time.
cards.why: It's a locals' favorite and the kitchen closes at 2, so this is the sweet spot.

cards.name: Cloud 9 Foot Spa San Jose - Formerly Oceanic
cards.address: 2989 Union Ave, San Jose, CA 95124, USA
cards.blurb: Treat yourself to a foot spa session at Cloud 9 in San Jose — unwind, decompress, and let someone else do the work for a couple of hours.
cards.why: A midday spa stop on a weekday feels genuinely indulgent.

cards.name: Arts and Culture Commission Meeting
cards.blurb: Sit in on the Arts and Culture Commission Meeting in Los Gatos — it's free, open to the public, and a great way to see what's shaping the local arts scene.
cards.why: Worth showing up if you care about what's happening culturally in town.

cards.name: West Valley Housing Resource Fair
cards.blurb: Stop by the West Valley Housing Resource Fair — free to attend and a genuinely useful community event even if you just want to stay informed.
cards.why: It's right in Los Gatos and wraps up the civic part of the day on a good note.

cards.name: Heritage Pub and Chop House
cards.address: 15 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at Heritage Pub and Chop House with a proper dinner and a drink on N Santa Cruz Ave — order something hearty and settle in.
cards.why: A solid dinner spot that keeps the whole day anchored in Los Gatos.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a walk along the creek trail. The path is flat, shaded, and easy for little legs, so let the kids run ahead while you wake up properly.
cards.why: Cool morning air and a peaceful trail is a perfect way to kick things off before the day gets busy.

cards.name: Heartbeat Cafe
cards.address: 14392 Union Ave, San Jose, CA 95124, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by Heartbeat Cafe for breakfast or a late morning bite. Good coffee, good food, and it closes at 3 PM so hit it while you can.
cards.why: A solid local cafe that hits the spot before a full afternoon of activities.

cards.name: Vasona Lake County Park
cards.address: 333 Blossom Hill Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Vasona Lake for the late morning stretch. The kids can check out the little train, run along the lake trail, and burn off all that breakfast energy. Grab a $6 parking spot and enjoy the open space.
cards.why: It's a classic family spot and the weather is perfect for being outside today.

cards.name: Saratoga Library
cards.address: 13650 Saratoga Ave, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Pop into Saratoga Library for a couple hours. Let the kids browse the shelves, pick out books, and settle into the reading area. It's a beautiful, calm space they'll actually enjoy.
cards.why: Free, air-conditioned, and genuinely fun for kids who love books or just need a chill midday break.

cards.name: Kids Knitting (Ages 7+)
cards.address: 110 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030
cards.blurb: Head to 110 E Main St for Kids Knitting at the library. Ages 7 and up, totally free, and a great hands-on activity to wrap up the afternoon.
cards.why: It's a free, drop-in creative activity that gives the kids something totally different to try today.

cards.name: Bronze Age Bat Cave
cards.address: 20 S Santa Cruz Ave # 112, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Walk over to Bronze Age Bat Cave for some browsing. It's a quirky, fun shop the kids will get a kick out of, full of pop culture collectibles and cool finds.
cards.why: Los Gatos has great walkable shops and this one is genuinely fun for kids and parents alike.

cards.name: Flowers Saratoga
cards.address: 14577 Big Basin Wy, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap the day with dinner at Flowers Saratoga on Big Basin Way. The setting is charming and the food is worth the trip out.
cards.why: A solid dinner pick to end a full day without going too far or spending too much.

cards.name: Ginzton Terrace Apartments
cards.address: 585 Franklin St, Mountain View, CA 94041
cards.blurb: Swing by the Ginzton Terrace Apartments community event — it's free and worth a quick look.
cards.why: Free, local, and fits perfectly between morning and lunch.

cards.name: Artisan Wine & Spirits
cards.address: 2495 Old Middlefield Way, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Browse Artisan Wine & Spirits on Old Middlefield — they carry a great selection of small-production wines and spirits, perfect for picking something fun to bring home.
cards.why: A bottle shop this good is worth the stop on any weekday when it's not crowded.

cards.name: OMESCAPE | Sunnyvale
cards.address: 1135 E Arques Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94085, USA
cards.blurb: Drop into OMESCAPE in Sunnyvale for an escape room session — they run high-quality rooms that are actually challenging and fun for adults.
cards.why: A great afternoon activity that keeps you engaged for a solid hour-plus.

cards.name: Crumbl
cards.address: 660B San Antonio Rd, Mountain View, CA 94040, USA
cards.blurb: Cap the day with a box of cookies from Crumbl on San Antonio — pick a couple of the rotating weekly flavors and call it dessert.
cards.why: The perfect low-key ending to a full day out.

cards.name: FireWorks Ceramics
cards.address: 483 Hope St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Try a drop-in pottery session at FireWorks Ceramics on Hope Street — even if you've never touched clay, it's a genuinely fun way to spend an afternoon.
cards.why: Creative, hands-on, and way more satisfying than just browsing shops.

cards.name: Manresa Bread
cards.address: 271 State St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Grab pastries and something warm to drink at Manresa Bread in Los Altos. The baked goods are genuinely worth waking up for.
cards.why: A cozy, low-key start that gets everyone fueled up before the day kicks off.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours at the Computer History Museum. The exhibits on how computers evolved from room-sized machines to pocket devices are genuinely wild, and kids tend to get hooked faster than you'd expect.
cards.why: It's engaging for all ages and the hands-on elements keep kids curious the whole time.

cards.name: Shoreline Park & Lake
cards.address: Shoreline at Mountain View
cards.blurb: Head outside to Shoreline Park for a walk around the lake and some fresh air. Let the kids run around and stretch after the museum.
cards.why: Perfect post-museum reset with big open space and bay views the whole family can enjoy.

cards.name: Sweet Shop
cards.address: 994 Los Altos Ave, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Stop into Sweet Shop in Los Altos for a sweet afternoon treat. Great spot to sit down and let everyone pick something they like.
cards.why: Kids plus a sweet shop is basically a guaranteed good mood.

cards.name: Cheeky Monkey Toys
cards.address: 173 Main St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Browse Cheeky Monkey Toys on Main Street in Los Altos. It's the kind of toy store that actually has interesting, thoughtful stuff rather than generic big-box finds.
cards.why: Kids love it and it's a genuinely fun browse even for adults tagging along.

cards.name: Page Turners Book Club for Grades 4-5
cards.blurb: Drop in for the Page Turners Book Club for Grades 4-5 at the Los Altos library. It's a free, welcoming group where kids talk about books with other readers their age.
cards.why: Free, social, and a great fit for any book-loving kid in that grade range.

cards.name: Dinah's Poolside Restaurant
cards.address: 4261 El Camino Real, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the day with dinner at Dinah's Poolside Restaurant in Palo Alto. Casual enough for kids and the poolside setting makes it feel a little special on a weeknight.
cards.why: A relaxed, fun dinner spot that holds up for families without breaking the budget too badly.

cards.name: Jessica Monette: Root Me in the Soil, In the Wake and the Becoming (Project Room Exhibition)
cards.blurb: This Project Room exhibition by Jessica Monette explores themes of rootedness and transformation through visual art — worth a slow, thoughtful look.
cards.why: Free art on a weekday morning is a pretty great way to ease into the day.

cards.name: Game Kastle (Santa Clara)
cards.address: 1350 Coleman Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Pick up a board game, browse the shelves, or join a table if something's going — Game Kastle is the kind of place you lose track of time in.
cards.why: Great way to stretch the afternoon into something social and low-key.

cards.name: Birre by Pour Decisions
cards.address: 320 Sharks Wy, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Settle in at Birre by Pour Decisions for craft beers and bites before the evening winds down.
cards.why: Low-key spot with solid drinks to cap off an active day.

cards.name: Little Wine House
cards.address: 350 W Julian St Bldg 4, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at Little Wine House with a glass or two in a cozy, intimate setting that feels nothing like a chain wine bar.
cards.why: It's a perfect Wednesday night wind-down spot.

cards.name: Sweetdragon Baking Company
cards.address: 898 Lincoln Ave, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Sweetdragon Baking Company with pastries and something warm. Great spot to fuel up the kids before the day kicks off.
cards.why: One of the best little bakeries in San Jose and a perfect low-key morning stop.

cards.name: Alum Rock Park
cards.address: 15350 Penitencia Creek Rd, San Jose, CA 95127, USA
cards.blurb: Head out to Alum Rock Park for a solid morning hike along Penitencia Creek. The trails are easy enough for kids and the canyon scenery is genuinely cool.
cards.why: Cool morning temps are perfect for getting outside before it warms up.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Hit the Santana Row Farmers Market for lunch. Walk the stalls, grab some food, let the kids pick something fun. It's a pretty easygoing scene.
cards.why: Fresh food, open air, and easy for families to graze at their own pace.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by the Triton Museum of Art for the free galleries and the sculpture garden out back. Kids can explore the outdoor space while you actually look at the art.
cards.why: Free admission and a sculpture garden make this totally doable with kids in tow.

cards.name: Rita's Italian Ice & Frozen Custard
cards.address: 1801 Pruneridge Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Stop at Rita's Italian Ice for a mid-afternoon treat. Grab an Italian ice or frozen custard and let the kids pick their flavor.
cards.why: Affordable, delicious, and the kids will love it after a museum afternoon.

cards.name: Mega Claws
cards.address: 979 Story Rd Unit 7027, San Jose, CA 95122, USA
cards.blurb: Head over to Mega Claws for some claw machine action. It's a whole arcade of claw machines and the kids will want to stay forever.
cards.why: Pure fun for kids and a totally different vibe to wind down the afternoon.

cards.name: The Dish Trail
cards.address: Stanford Foothills (Stanford Ave trailhead)
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a hike up The Dish Trail. Take the full loop for those sweeping views of the Bay and the Santa Cruz Mountains — it earns the payoff.
cards.why: Cool morning temps make this the perfect window before it warms up.

cards.name: Evvia Estiatorio
cards.address: 420 Emerson St, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Grab a proper lunch at Evvia, a Greek spot on Emerson. Order something from the wood-fired section of the menu and sit inside — the room feels like a taverna.
cards.why: You just hiked, you've earned a real lunch at a genuinely good restaurant.

cards.name: Cantor Arts Center
cards.address: Cantor Arts Center, Stanford University
cards.blurb: Walk the Cantor Arts Center on Stanford's campus. The Rodin Sculpture Garden out front is free and completely open, and the galleries inside rotate enough to always have something worth slowing down for.
cards.why: Free world-class art on a cool afternoon is hard to beat.

cards.name: Blue Danube Spa
cards.address: 200 California Ave suite 170, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down at Blue Danube Spa on California Ave. Book a massage or body treatment in advance — this is the kind of place you leave feeling like a different person.
cards.why: A Wednesday afternoon spa visit feels indulgent in the best way.

cards.name: La Bodeguita del Medio
cards.address: 463 California Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Stop into La Bodeguita del Medio on California Ave for a mojito and some food before the evening. The Cuban food is solid and the vibe is warm and a little festive.
cards.why: Great spot to ease into the evening before heading to the book event.

cards.name: Kelly Yang with Mychal Threets
cards.address: 1010 El Camino Real, Menlo Park, CA, 94025
cards.blurb: Head to Kepler's Books in Menlo Park for Kelly Yang and Mychal Threets in conversation. Get there a little early to grab a good seat — these tend to fill up.
cards.why: This is a genuinely fun author event and a great way to cap the day.

cards.name: Basquia Bakery
cards.address: 1775 E Bayshore Rd, East Palo Alto, CA 94303, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Basquia Bakery — grab pastries and coffee for the grown-ups while the kids pick out something sweet to kick off the day.
cards.why: Best way to start a Wednesday is with something fresh from a great local bakery.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours at the Computer History Museum — the exhibits are way more fascinating than they sound, and kids genuinely get sucked into the hands-on displays and wild stories about where computers came from.
cards.why: It hits that sweet spot where kids think it's cool and parents actually learn something too.

cards.name: Simply Sabor
cards.address: 340 Castro St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Simply Sabor on Castro Street for lunch. Order the Mexican street food classics and let the kids go wild with the flavors.
cards.why: Casual, affordable, and genuinely delicious — perfect midday fuel for the family.

cards.name: Cantor Arts Center
cards.address: Cantor Arts Center, Stanford University
cards.blurb: Take the kids through Cantor Arts Center on the Stanford campus. The outdoor Rodin sculptures alone are worth it, and the free galleries inside give you plenty to explore without rushing.
cards.why: Free world-class art on a beautiful campus makes for a surprisingly great family afternoon.

cards.name: Family Craft: Yarn Weaving
cards.address: 1276  Harriet Street  Palo Alto
cards.blurb: Drop into the Family Craft: Yarn Weaving event at 1276 Harriet Street. Kids get to make something with their hands and take it home, which is always a win.
cards.why: Free, creative, and the kids will love having something to show for it at the end of the day.

cards.name: Fish With You鱼你在一起Mountain View
cards.address: 156 Castro St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap up the evening with dinner at Fish With You on Castro Street in Mountain View. Order the fish rice bowls and let the kids try something a little different.
cards.why: Solid flavors, easy on the wallet, and a fun spot the whole family will enjoy.
